abstract_skeleton: We present STWM, a trace-first semantic world model for future identity association. A frozen future trace backbone provides physical continuity, while TUSB-v3.1 binds semantic trace units to identity-conditioned state. A trace_belief_assoc readout combines trace prior, semantic evidence, unit identity, and coordinate plausibility to reduce confuser errors and improve occlusion-aware reacquisition and planning-lite risk utilities. We report bounded improvements over internal baselines and clarify external baseline boundaries; we do not claim full video generation, closed-loop planning, or external overall SOTA.
appendix_structure: FutureSemanticTraceState diagnostic and negative V7 result
introduction_bullet_logic: Future identity association is hard under occlusion, long gaps, confusers, and OOD shifts.
Appearance-only or teacher-only matching can select visually similar future confusers.
Trace continuity provides physical identity evidence but needs semantic binding.
STWM binds semantic trace units to trace-conditioned belief state.
The paper evaluates association, false-confuser reduction, reacquisition, risk utility, and counterfactual trace interventions with explicit claim boundaries.
limitation_section: Not external overall SOTA; SAM2/CoTracker stronger overall on external diagnostic.
Appearance-change and universal OOD dominance are not solved.
FutureSemanticTraceState explicit branch remains appendix diagnostic after V7.
method_structure: Utility readouts: reacquisition and planning-lite risk
Claim boundary: what STWM is not
preferred_title: STWM: A Semantic-Trace World Model with Belief Filtering for Future Identity Association
related_work_structure: video object segmentation and point tracking baselines
title_options: STWM: A Semantic-Trace World Model with Belief Filtering for Future Identity Association
Semantic Trace World Models for Future Identity Association
Trace-Conditioned Semantic Belief for Occlusion-Aware Future Association
